turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, I was reading this article about kids these days playing video games all night instead of helping with chores or learning real skills. Don't you think that's messing up society? Back in our grandparents' time, things were different.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, A, totally. My grandpa always said, 'Idle hands make the devil's work.' Kids need to be out working the land or learning a trade, like our ancestors did. No screens, just real life.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly! Tradition says family comes first. Remember what our elders taught? 'The village raises the child,' but now everyone's glued to phones. Uh, it's like we're forgetting how to live right.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Spot on. Ancestral wisdom from the old proverbs: 'As the twig is bent, so grows the tree.' If we don't bend 'em right with chores and respect, they're gonna grow crooked. My dad drilled that into us.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Haha, yeah, my mom would quote that all the time. And think about marriage too – kids jumping into relationships without waiting, cohabiting and all. Our traditions say wait for the elders' blessing and a proper ceremony. That's why divorces were rare back then.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Um, true, but... wait, weren't there arranged marriages? Anyway, the elders knew best. 'Honor thy father and mother,' straight from the old ways. Modern dating apps? Nah, that's chaos. Ancestors had it figured out.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Right, and education – why all this college debt for jobs that might not exist? Our grandparents learned by doing, apprenticing. Tradition: 'Knowledge from the soil and the sweat.' No need for fancy degrees.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Totally, A. Tangent, but speaking of soil, look at farming. Chemicals everywhere now, but ancestors rotated crops and used natural stuff. Their wisdom kept the land fertile for generations. We're poisoning it!

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Whoa, good point. My uncle still follows the old lunar calendar for planting, says it's from our great-great-grandparents. Works every time. Why mess with what's been proven for centuries?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hes, yeah... proven by survival, right? If it wasn't wise, we wouldn't be here. Like gender roles – men hunt, women nurture, as per ancient roles. Modern 'equality' is just confusing everyone.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly, B. Elders said, 'A place for everything, everything in its place.' That's harmony. Now it's all mixed up, and look at the mental health crisis. Ancestral ways kept us balanced.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Balance, yeah. One more: discipline. Spare the rod, spoil the child – old school, but it worked. No therapy bills back then. Traditions build tough people.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Haha, true, though my grandma was gentle about it. But yeah, the wisdom's there. We should revive more of it instead of this new-age nonsense.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: For sure. Our ancestors survived wars, famines – they knew. We ignore 'em at our peril.

input.objective: Defending traditional lifestyles and discipline by citing ancestral wisdom and proverbs against modern habits
